title: Slash Commands in the SDK
description: Learn how to use slash commands to control Claude Code sessions through the SDK

breadcrumb: Slash Commands in the SDK > Overview
text:
> Learn how to use slash commands to control Claude Code sessions through the SDK

Slash commands provide a way to control Claude Code sessions with special commands that start with `/`. These commands can be sent through the SDK to perform actions like compacting context, listing context usage, or invoking custom commands. Only commands that work without an interactive terminal are dispatchable through the SDK; the `system/init` message lists the ones available in your session.

preceding_paragraph: The Claude Agent SDK provides information about available slash commands in the system initialization message. Access this information when your session starts:

text:
import asyncio
from claude_agent_sdk import query, ClaudeAgentOptions, SystemMessage

async def main():
    async for message in query(prompt="Hello Claude", options=ClaudeAgentOptions(max_turns=1)):
        if isinstance(message, SystemMessage) and message.subtype == "init":
            print("Available slash commands:", message.data["slash_commands"])
            # Example output: ["/compact", "/context", "/usage"]

asyncio.run(main())
preceding_paragraph: The Claude Agent SDK provides information about available slash commands in the system initialization message. Access this information when your session starts:

breadcrumb: Slash Commands in the SDK > Discovering Available Slash Commands
text: The Claude Agent SDK provides information about available slash commands in the system initialization message. Access this information when your session starts:

preceding_paragraph: Send slash commands by including them in your prompt string, just like regular text:

text:
import asyncio
from claude_agent_sdk import query, ClaudeAgentOptions, ResultMessage

async def main():
    # Send a slash command
    async for message in query(prompt="/compact", options=ClaudeAgentOptions(max_turns=1)):
        if isinstance(message, ResultMessage):
            print("Command executed:", message.result)

asyncio.run(main())
preceding_paragraph: Send slash commands by including them in your prompt string, just like regular text:

breadcrumb: Slash Commands in the SDK > Sending Slash Commands
text: Send slash commands by including them in your prompt string, just like regular text:

preceding_paragraph: The `/compact` command reduces the size of your conversation history by summarizing older messages while preserving important context:

text:
import asyncio
from claude_agent_sdk import query, ClaudeAgentOptions, SystemMessage

async def main():
    async for message in query(prompt="/compact", options=ClaudeAgentOptions(max_turns=1)):
        if isinstance(message, SystemMessage) and message.subtype == "compact_boundary":
            print("Compaction completed")
            print("Pre-compaction tokens:", message.data["compact_metadata"]["pre_tokens"])
            print("Trigger:", message.data["compact_metadata"]["trigger"])

asyncio.run(main())
preceding_paragraph: The `/compact` command reduces the size of your conversation history by summarizing older messages while preserving important context:

breadcrumb: Slash Commands in the SDK > Common Slash Commands > `/compact` - Compact Conversation History
text: The `/compact` command reduces the size of your conversation history by summarizing older messages while preserving important context:

breadcrumb: Slash Commands in the SDK > Common Slash Commands > Clearing the conversation
text: The interactive `/clear` command is not available in the SDK. Each `query()` call already starts a fresh conversation, so to clear context, end the current `query()` and start a new one. The previous conversation stays on disk and can be returned to by passing its session ID to the `resume` option.

breadcrumb: Slash Commands in the SDK > Common Slash Commands

text: The `.claude/commands/` directory is the legacy format. The recommended format is `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md`, which supports the same slash-command invocation (`/name`) plus autonomous invocation by Claude. See Skills for the current format. The CLI continues to support both formats, and the examples below remain accurate for `.claude/commands/`.

breadcrumb: Slash Commands in the SDK > Creating Custom Slash Commands > File Locations
text:
Custom slash commands are stored in designated directories based on their scope:

- Project commands: `.claude/commands/` - Available only in the current project (legacy; prefer `.claude/skills/`)
- Personal commands: `~/.claude/commands/` - Available across all your projects (legacy; prefer `~/.claude/skills/`)

Refactor the selected code to improve readability and maintainability.
Focus on clean code principles and best practices.
preceding_paragraph: Create `.claude/commands/refactor.md`:

breadcrumb: Slash Commands in the SDK > Creating Custom Slash Commands > File Format > Basic Example
text:
Create `.claude/commands/refactor.md`:

This creates the `/refactor` command that you can use through the SDK.

---
allowed-tools: Read, Grep, Glob
description: Run security vulnerability scan
model: claude-opus-4-7
---

Analyze the codebase for security vulnerabilities including:
- SQL injection risks
- XSS vulnerabilities
- Exposed credentials
- Insecure configurations
preceding_paragraph: Create `.claude/commands/security-check.md`:

breadcrumb: Slash Commands in the SDK > Creating Custom Slash Commands > File Format > With Frontmatter
text: Create `.claude/commands/security-check.md`:

breadcrumb: Slash Commands in the SDK > Creating Custom Slash Commands > File Format
text:
Each custom command is a markdown file where:

- The filename (without `.md` extension) becomes the command name
- The file content defines what the command does
- Optional YAML frontmatter provides configuration

preceding_paragraph: Once defined in the filesystem, custom commands are automatically available through the SDK:

text:
import asyncio
from claude_agent_sdk import query, ClaudeAgentOptions, AssistantMessage, SystemMessage

async def main():
    # Use a custom command
    async for message in query(
        prompt="/refactor src/auth/login.py", options=ClaudeAgentOptions(max_turns=3)
    ):
        if isinstance(message, AssistantMessage):
            for block in message.content:
                if hasattr(block, "text"):
                    print("Refactoring suggestions:", block.text)

    # Custom commands appear in the slash_commands list
    async for message in query(prompt="Hello", options=ClaudeAgentOptions(max_turns=1)):
        if isinstance(message, SystemMessage) and message.subtype == "init":
            # Will include both built-in and custom commands
            print("Available commands:", message.data["slash_commands"])
            # Example: ["/compact", "/context", "/usage", "/refactor", "/security-check"]

asyncio.run(main())
preceding_paragraph: Once defined in the filesystem, custom commands are automatically available through the SDK:

breadcrumb: Slash Commands in the SDK > Creating Custom Slash Commands > Using Custom Commands in the SDK
text: Once defined in the filesystem, custom commands are automatically available through the SDK:

---
argument-hint: [issue-number] [priority]
description: Fix a GitHub issue
---

Fix issue #$1 with priority $2.
Check the issue description and implement the necessary changes.
preceding_paragraph: Create `.claude/commands/fix-issue.md`:
Use in SDK:

text:
import asyncio
from claude_agent_sdk import query, ClaudeAgentOptions, ResultMessage

async def main():
    # Pass arguments to custom command
    async for message in query(prompt="/fix-issue 123 high", options=ClaudeAgentOptions(max_turns=5)):
        # Command will process with $1="123" and $2="high"
        if isinstance(message, ResultMessage):
            print("Issue fixed:", message.result)

asyncio.run(main())
preceding_paragraph: Use in SDK:

breadcrumb: Slash Commands in the SDK > Creating Custom Slash Commands > Advanced Features > Arguments and Placeholders
text:
Custom commands support dynamic arguments using placeholders:

Create `.claude/commands/fix-issue.md`:

Use in SDK:

---
allowed-tools: Bash(git add *), Bash(git status *), Bash(git commit *)
description: Create a git commit
---

## Context

- Current status: !`git status`
- Current diff: !`git diff HEAD`

## Task

Create a git commit with appropriate message based on the changes.
preceding_paragraph: Create `.claude/commands/git-commit.md`:

Custom commands can execute bash commands and include their output:

Review the following configuration files for issues:
- Package config: @package.json
- TypeScript config: @tsconfig.json

Check for security issues, outdated dependencies, and misconfigurations.

text:
.claude/commands/
├── frontend/
│   ├── component.md      # Creates /component (project:frontend)
│   └── style-check.md     # Creates /style-check (project:frontend)
├── backend/
│   ├── api-test.md        # Creates /api-test (project:backend)
│   └── db-migrate.md      # Creates /db-migrate (project:backend)
└── review.md              # Creates /review (project)
preceding_paragraph: Organize commands in subdirectories for better structure:

breadcrumb: Slash Commands in the SDK > Creating Custom Slash Commands > Organization with Namespacing
text:
Organize commands in subdirectories for better structure:

The subdirectory appears in the command description but doesn't affect the command name itself.

---
allowed-tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash(git diff *)
description: Comprehensive code review
---

## Changed Files
!`git diff --name-only HEAD~1`

## Detailed Changes
!`git diff HEAD~1`

## Review Checklist

Review the above changes for:
1. Code quality and readability
2. Security vulnerabilities
3. Performance implications
4. Test coverage
5. Documentation completeness

Provide specific, actionable feedback organized by priority.
preceding_paragraph: Create `.claude/commands/code-review.md`:

breadcrumb: Slash Commands in the SDK > Creating Custom Slash Commands > Practical Examples > Code Review Command
text: Create `.claude/commands/code-review.md`:

---
allowed-tools: Bash, Read, Edit
argument-hint: [test-pattern]
description: Run tests with optional pattern
---

Run tests matching pattern: $ARGUMENTS

1. Detect the test framework (Jest, pytest, etc.)
2. Run tests with the provided pattern
3. If tests fail, analyze and fix them
4. Re-run to verify fixes
preceding_paragraph: Create `.claude/commands/test.md`:
Use these commands through the SDK:

text:
import asyncio
from claude_agent_sdk import query, ClaudeAgentOptions

async def main():
    # Run code review
    async for message in query(prompt="/code-review", options=ClaudeAgentOptions(max_turns=3)):
        # Process review feedback
        pass

    # Run specific tests
    async for message in query(prompt="/test auth", options=ClaudeAgentOptions(max_turns=5)):
        # Handle test results
        pass

asyncio.run(main())

Slash Commands in the SDK > Creating Custom Slash Commands
text: In addition to using built-in slash commands, you can create your own custom commands that are available through the SDK. Custom commands are defined as markdown files in specific directories, similar to how subagents are configured.
